---
title: "Slender Goldentop (Euthamia caroliniana)"
slug: "euthamia-caroliniana"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622655955/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Slender Goldentop"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622655955/medium.jpg" alt="Slender Goldentop" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/marykeim">
marykeim</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341973112">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=129032)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and flat-topped clusters of small yellow flower heads. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and marsh edges. It typically blooms in late summer to fall and provides an important nectar source for a variety of pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/129032-Euthamia-caroliniana)
-   [Local profile](https://www.fnps.org/plant/euthamia#gsc.tab=0)
